name: Jack Bech
headline: Buried in the pecking order, but the talent's real
body:
Bech's rookie line tells the story of a player who never got off the bench: 39.8% snaps, a 32.5% route share, and just 29 targets across 14 games for a thin 5.9% target share and a 0.14 WOPR. The efficiency followed the usage down, with -0.07 EPA per play, a 48.3% success rate, and a meager 1.2 yards per route run. He caught 69% of his looks for 224 yards and zero scores, hardly the breakout TCU hype suggested.

The path forward runs through volume he doesn't yet have. He sits behind Brock Bowers, Tre Tucker, and Jalen Nailor in the target queue, and the quarterback picture, Kirk Cousins or a rookie, is unsettled under Klint Kubiak. Drafted around WR100 with projections near 41-483-2, he's a late-round dart worth a flier. Just understand you're betting on a role he has to win, not one he holds.
